documentation.file_docstring: Query expansion for FTS-only search mode. When no embedding provider is available, we fall back to FTS (full-text search). FTS works best with specific keywords, but users often ask conversational queries like "that thing we discussed yesterday" or "之前讨论的那个方案". This module extracts meaningful keywords from such queries to improve FTS results.
inline_comments: Common stop words that don't add search value
Time references (vague, not useful for FTS)
Common Korean trailing particles to strip from words for tokenization
Sorted by descending length so longest-match-first is guaranteed.
Prevent bogus one-syllable stems from words like "논의" -> "논".
Keep stripped ASCII stems for mixed tokens like "API를" -> "api".
Check if a token looks like a meaningful keyword. Returns false for short tokens, numbers-only, etc.
Skip very short English words (likely stop words or fragments)
Skip pure numbers (not useful for semantic search)
Skip tokens that are all punctuation

Simple tokenizer that handles English, Chinese, Korean, and Japanese text. For Chinese, we do character-based splitting since we don't have a proper segmenter. For English, we split on whitespace and punctuation.
Split into segments (English words, Chinese character sequences, etc.)
Japanese text often mixes scripts (kanji/kana/ASCII) without spaces.
Extract script-specific chunks so technical terms like "API" / "バグ" are retained.
Check if segment contains CJK characters (Chinese)
For Chinese, extract character n-grams (unigrams and bigrams)
Add bigrams for better phrase matching
For Korean (Hangul syllables and jamo), keep the word as-is unless it is
effectively a stop word once trailing particles are removed.
Also emit particle-stripped stems when they are useful keywords.
For non-CJK, keep as single token

Extract keywords from a conversational query for FTS search. Examples: - "that thing we discussed about the API" → ["discussed", "API"] - "之前讨论的那个方案" → ["讨论", "方案"] - "what was the solution for the bug" → ["solution", "bug"]
Expand a query for FTS search. Returns both the original query and extracted keywords for OR-matching. @param query - User's original query @returns Object with original query and extracted keywords
Build expanded query: original terms OR extracted keywords
This ensures both exact matches and keyword matches are found
Type for an optional LLM-based query expander. Can be provided to enhance keyword extraction with semantic understanding.
Expand query with optional LLM assistance. Falls back to local extraction if LLM is unavailable or fails.
If LLM expander is provided, try it first
LLM failed, fall back to local extraction
Fall back to local keyword extraction
